title: LSP server — core infrastructure
analysis:
The reify-lsp crate is currently a stub containing only a comment. Task 32 requires building core LSP infrastructure: initialization, document sync (didOpen/didChange/didClose), and diagnostics publishing. The architecture doc (section 3.9) mandates tower-lsp. The existing pipeline is: source → reify_syntax::parse() → reify_compiler::compile() → reify_eval::Engine::check(). Diagnostics come from three layers: ParseError (with SourceSpan), compiler Diagnostic (with DiagnosticLabel/SourceSpan), and eval CheckResult diagnostics. All use byte-offset SourceSpan which must be converted to LSP line/column Positions. The Engine is not Send (holds Box<dyn ConstraintChecker> etc.), so it must be managed behind a tokio Mutex or on a dedicated task. For initial LSP, we only need check (no geometry kernel), so Engine::new(checker, None) suffices. The CLI already has check/build commands and needs a new 'lsp' subcommand that starts the server on stdio. Key integration points: reify-test-support provides bracket_source(), MockConstraintChecker for testing the pipeline without real geometry.

POST-REVIEW UPDATE: Reviewer identified a blocking panic in offset_to_position when byte offsets land mid-character in multi-byte UTF-8 sequences. Tree-sitter error-recovery spans routinely produce such offsets. Steps 21-22 added to fix this with TDD.

prerequisites.description: Add tower-lsp and serde_json workspace dependencies to root Cargo.toml. Add tower-lsp = { version = "0.20", features = ["proposed"] } and serde_json = "1" to [workspace.dependencies].

Update crates/reify-lsp/Cargo.toml: add dependencies on reify-types, reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-eval, reify-constraints (for SimpleConstraintChecker), tower-lsp, tokio, serde_json. Add [dev-dependencies] on reify-test-support.
Update crates/reify-cli/Cargo.toml: add dependencies on reify-lsp and tokio (for async main in lsp subcommand).

steps.description: Write failing tests for byte-offset SourceSpan → LSP Position conversion. Test cases: (1) offset 0 in empty string → Position(0,0), (2) offset within first line → correct column, (3) offset on second line → line 1 with correct column, (4) offset at newline character, (5) multi-line source with bracket_source() fixture verifying known span positions. Place tests in crates/reify-lsp/src/convert.rs as #[cfg(test)] mod tests.

Implement convert module: fn offset_to_position(source: &str, offset: u32) -> lsp_types::Position and fn span_to_range(source: &str, span: SourceSpan) -> lsp_types::Range. Iterate source bytes counting newlines to derive line/character. Handle UTF-16 offset encoding per LSP spec (tower-lsp default).

Write failing tests for Reify Diagnostic → LSP Diagnostic conversion. Test cases: (1) Severity::Error → DiagnosticSeverity::ERROR, (2) Severity::Warning → WARNING, (3) Severity::Info → INFORMATION, (4) Diagnostic with no labels → range at (0,0), (5) Diagnostic with one label → range from label's span, (6) Diagnostic with multiple labels → primary span from first label, related info from rest. Use a sample source string for position resolution.

Implement fn convert_diagnostic(diag: &reify_types::Diagnostic, source: &str, uri: &lsp_types::Url) -> lsp_types::Diagnostic. Maps severity, extracts range from first label span, converts remaining labels to DiagnosticRelatedInformation. Also implement fn convert_parse_error(err: &ParseError, source: &str, uri: &Url) -> lsp_types::Diagnostic for parse errors.

Write failing tests for DocumentStore: (1) open_document stores text and URI, (2) get_document returns text after open, (3) update_document replaces text (full sync), (4) close_document removes entry, (5) get_document returns None after close, (6) open two documents independently. Place in crates/reify-lsp/src/document.rs.

Implement DocumentStore struct with HashMap<Url, DocumentState>. DocumentState holds { text: String, version: i32 }. Methods: open(uri, text, version), update(uri, text, version), close(uri), get(uri) -> Option<&DocumentState>. Pure data structure, no async.

Write failing tests for the diagnostics pipeline function. Test cases: (1) valid bracket source → no error-severity diagnostics, (2) source with syntax error (e.g., 'structure {') → at least one parse error diagnostic, (3) source with unknown identifier → unresolved name diagnostic, (4) empty source → no crash, returns empty diagnostics. Use MockConstraintChecker from reify-test-support.

Implement fn compute_diagnostics(source: &str, uri: &Url) -> Vec<lsp_types::Diagnostic>. Internally: parse source with reify_syntax::parse, compile with reify_compiler::compile, run engine.check() with SimpleConstraintChecker, collect all parse errors + compiler diagnostics + eval diagnostics, convert each via convert module. Return collected LSP diagnostics.

Write failing test for LSP server initialization. Create a ReifyLanguageServer, call initialize() with an InitializeParams, verify the returned InitializeResult includes: TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL in capabilities, diagnostic provider capability is set. Use tower-lsp test utilities or direct struct construction.

Implement ReifyLanguageServer struct holding Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<ServerState>> where ServerState contains DocumentStore. Implement LanguageServer trait initialize() returning ServerCapabilities with text_document_sync: Some(TextDocumentSyncCapability::Kind(TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL)) and diagnostic_provider. Also implement initialized() and shutdown().

Write failing test for did_open handler: opening a document with valid bracket source triggers publish_diagnostics with the document URI and a diagnostics list. Use a mock/spy client or verify DocumentStore state after did_open. Verify parse-compile-check pipeline ran and diagnostics were computed.

Implement LanguageServer::did_open(): extract URI and text from DidOpenTextDocumentParams, store in DocumentStore, spawn compute_diagnostics on a tokio task, publish results via self.client.publish_diagnostics(uri, diagnostics, version).

Write failing test for did_change handler: after opening a document, changing its content triggers a new diagnostics computation. Change from valid source to source with an error and verify new diagnostics are published. Also test that DocumentStore reflects the updated text.
Implement LanguageServer::did_change(): extract URI and new text from DidChangeTextDocumentParams (full sync — take last content change), update DocumentStore, recompute and publish diagnostics.

Write failing test for did_close handler: after closing a document, DocumentStore no longer has the entry. Verify that publish_diagnostics is called with empty diagnostics list to clear editor squiggles.
Implement LanguageServer::did_close(): remove document from DocumentStore, publish empty diagnostics to clear the editor's diagnostic display for the closed file.

Write failing test for CLI 'reify lsp' subcommand: verify that the main() function recognizes 'lsp' as a valid command (does not return 'Unknown command'). This can be tested by checking the match arm exists. Also verify the help text includes the lsp command.

Add 'lsp' command to reify-cli main.rs: match 'lsp' arm that calls reify_lsp::run_server() (or similar). Implement pub async fn run_server() in reify-lsp/src/lib.rs that creates the ReifyLanguageServer, builds the tower-lsp Service, and serves over stdin/stdout via Server::new(stdin, stdout, socket).serve(service). The CLI wraps this in a tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().block_on() call.

Write integration test in crates/reify-lsp/tests/ that exercises the full LSP lifecycle: initialize → did_open with bracket source → verify diagnostics → did_change with broken source → verify error diagnostics → did_close. Use tower-lsp's test client or simulate JSON-RPC messages.

Fix any issues found in integration testing. Wire up the public API in lib.rs: pub mod convert, pub mod document, pub mod server. Export run_server as the main entry point. Ensure cargo test passes for all crates, cargo clippy is clean.

Write failing tests for UTF-8 boundary safety in offset_to_position (review fix: panic_on_invalid_utf8_slice). In crates/reify-lsp/src/convert.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests, add: (1) offset_mid_multibyte_2byte: source contains a 2-byte char (e.g., "a\u{00E9}b" = "aéb"), pass offset pointing to the continuation byte (byte index 2, which is 0x80..0xBF) — must not panic, should snap forward to the next char boundary and return a valid Position. (2) offset_mid_multibyte_3byte: source "a\u{4E16}b" ("a世b"), offset at byte 2 and byte 3 (middle of the 3-byte 世) — must not panic. (3) offset_mid_multibyte_4byte: source with a 4-byte emoji "a\u{1F600}b" ("a😀b"), offsets at bytes 2, 3, 4 — must not panic. (4) offset_at_exact_char_boundary: source "aéb", offset at byte 0, 1, 3, 4 (all valid boundaries) — returns correct positions, confirming no regression. (5) offset_mid_char_in_second_line: source "x\n\u{00E9}y", offset pointing mid-é on line 2 — must not panic, line number must be 1. (6) span_to_range_mid_multibyte: a SourceSpan where start or end lands mid-character — must not panic, range should be valid. These tests should panic/fail against the current implementation, proving the bug exists.

Fix offset_to_position in crates/reify-lsp/src/convert.rs to handle byte offsets that land mid-character in multi-byte UTF-8 sequences. After computing `clamped = offset.min(bytes.len())`, snap `clamped` forward to the next valid UTF-8 character boundary: `while clamped < bytes.len() && !source.is_char_boundary(clamped) { clamped += 1; }`. Apply the same snapping to `line_start` for defensive safety (though `line_start` is always after a `\n` byte, which is a valid boundary). The line_slice `&source[line_start..clamped]` is now guaranteed to be valid UTF-8. Also snap `line_start` forward if it somehow lands mid-char (defensive). This ensures Tree-sitter error-recovery spans pointing to mid-character byte positions never crash the LSP server. All step-21 tests must pass, and all existing tests must continue to pass with no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Use full document sync (TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL) instead of incremental
design_decisions.rationale: Simpler to implement and correct. Incremental sync requires tracking edits and applying patches to stored text. Full sync means we always have the complete source text. Performance is fine for typical .ri files (small files). Can upgrade to incremental sync later if needed.

design_decisions.decision: Run diagnostics computation synchronously within the LSP handler (no background debounce)
design_decisions.rationale: The parse → compile → check pipeline is fast for single-file .ri files (sub-millisecond for small files). A debounce mechanism adds complexity. We can add debouncing in task 33 or a follow-up if latency becomes an issue.

design_decisions.decision: Use Engine::check() without geometry kernel for LSP diagnostics
design_decisions.rationale: LSP diagnostics only need parse errors, compile diagnostics, and constraint satisfaction status. Geometry realization is expensive and not needed for editor feedback. Engine::new(checker, None) skips geometry entirely.

design_decisions.decision: Create a fresh Engine per diagnostics computation rather than maintaining a persistent one
design_decisions.rationale: Simplifies state management — no need to worry about Engine state accumulating across edits. The Engine's incremental evaluation (edit_param) is designed for parameter tweaks within a stable topology. Source text changes require full re-parse/compile anyway, invalidating the entire eval graph. Persistent Engine with incremental re-eval can be added in a future task.

design_decisions.decision: ServerState behind Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<>> for interior mutability
design_decisions.rationale: tower-lsp requires the LanguageServer to be Send + Sync. RwLock allows concurrent reads (e.g., multiple hover requests) and exclusive writes (document updates). tokio::sync::RwLock is used over std because tower-lsp handlers are async.

design_decisions.decision: Place conversion utilities in a separate convert module
design_decisions.rationale: Byte-offset → Position conversion and Diagnostic conversion are pure functions with no dependencies on server state. Separating them enables thorough unit testing without needing LSP server infrastructure.

design_decisions.decision: CLI uses synchronous tokio Runtime::new().block_on() for lsp subcommand
design_decisions.rationale: The existing CLI main() is synchronous. Rather than converting the entire CLI to async, only the lsp subcommand needs async. block_on() bridges sync → async cleanly. The other commands (check, build) remain synchronous.

design_decisions.decision: Snap mid-character byte offsets forward to next char boundary (not backward)
design_decisions.rationale: When a byte offset falls inside a continuation bytes of a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence, we snap forward to the next character boundary rather than backward. Forward-snapping is simpler (just advance while !is_char_boundary) and avoids the risk of snapping backward past a newline, which would corrupt line counting. Using str::is_char_boundary() is the idiomatic Rust approach and handles all UTF-8 sequence lengths (2/3/4 byte) uniformly. This matches the reviewer's suggestion of advancing past continuation bytes (bytes matching 0x80..0xBF pattern).

reuse.how: Call parse(source, ModulePath::single(name)) to get ParsedModule with errors and declarations from source text. Same API used by CLI.
Call compile(&parsed_module) to get CompiledModule with diagnostics. Same API used by CLI.
Create Engine::new(checker, None), call check(&compiled) to get CheckResult with values, constraint_results, and diagnostics. Same pattern as CLI cmd_check.
Use as the ConstraintChecker implementation for the LSP engine, same as CLI.
These are the internal diagnostic types that the convert module translates to lsp_types::Diagnostic. SourceSpan byte offsets are converted to LSP line/column positions.
ParsedModule.errors contains parse errors with SourceSpan. Convert these to LSP diagnostics alongside compiler diagnostics.
Use in LSP tests as a known-good .ri source to verify the diagnostics pipeline produces no errors for valid code.
Use in unit tests for the diagnostics pipeline to avoid depending on SimpleConstraintChecker behavior.
The LSP diagnostics pipeline follows the same parse → compile → engine pattern. Reuse the same sequencing: parse source, forward parse errors, compile, collect diagnostics.
Use in the UTF-8 boundary fix to test whether a byte index is a valid character boundary. More idiomatic and robust than manually checking continuation byte patterns (0x80..0xBF).
